chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail App. No. S – 307 of 2024 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Hearing of bail application 1. For orders on office objection at Flag-A 2. For hearing of bail application 15.07.2024 Mr. Shoaib Niaz Khaskheli, Advocate along with applicants. Mr. Shafi Muhammad Mahar, Deputy Prosecutor General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- ZULFIQAR ALI SANGI, J. – Applicants seek pre-arrest bail in FIR No.205/2023, registered at Police Station Faiz Ganj, District Khairpur for offences under Sections 365-B, 344, PPC, wherein they were booked for alleged abduction of Mst. Nazia. Their earlier pre-arrest bail application was rejected by learned Additional Sessions Judge-IV / Gender Based Violence Court, Khairpur vide order dated 20.12.2023. 2. Heard learned Counsel for the applicants and learned Deputy Prosecutor General, and perused the material available on record with their able assistance. 3. From the tentative assessment of the record, it reflects that the abductee was recovered from the house of Abdul Fattah while she was sewing clothes. Co-accused Muhammad Jurial, whose pre-arrest bail application was earlier declined by the trial Court along with present applicants, has been granted post-arrest bail by learned Additional Sessions Judge-IV / Special Judge for GBV, Khairpur vide order dated 06.06.2024. The case of applicants is on better footing to that of co-accused. 4. Learned Deputy Prosecutor General, while going through the bail granting order of co-accused Muhammad Jurial, has conceded for confirmation of bail of the applicants. Cr. Bail App. No. S – 307 of 2024 Page 2 of 2 5. Under such circumstances, applicants have made out their case for confirmation of bail.

S – 307 of 2024 Page 2 of 2 5. Under such circumstances, applicants have made out their case for confirmation of bail. Therefore, this application is allowed, and ad-interim pre-arrest bail already granted to the applicants vide order dated 16.05.2024 is hereby confirmed on the same terms and conditions. 6. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and the learned trial Court shall not be influenced by this order in any manner whatsoever, while deciding the case on merit. Instant bail application is accordingly disposed of. J U D G E Abdul Basit
